australia.title: Public transport & driving, city by city
australia.lead: Weekly commute costs below are fictional planning bands (single zone or modest drive) — tune them to your suburb and campus or office postcode.
australia.disclaimer: Verify fares, tolls, and licence recognition with state transport authorities before you rely on any figure here.
australia.licenceBlurb: Each state sets how long you may drive on a valid overseas licence before requiring a local licence. Heavy vehicles and medical standards add extra rules.

officialLinks.label: NSW — driving on an overseas licence

cities.name: Sydney
cities.modeMix: Train, light rail, bus, ferry on Opal
cities.licenceNote: Check NSW recognition and 3-month rules for your country.

cities.name: Melbourne
cities.licenceNote: Hook turns and tram-only lanes surprise newcomers—practice off-peak.

cities.name: Brisbane
cities.licenceNote: Toll tunnels add up—map M1/M3 if driving daily.

cities.name: Perth
cities.modeMix: Long north–south rail spines + bus feeders
cities.licenceNote: Coastal sprawl means distances grow quickly—fuel budget matters.

new-zealand.title: Ferries, buses, and honest distances
new-zealand.lead: Illustrative weekly spend on a mix of buses and occasional fuel — Auckland ferries can lift totals fast.
new-zealand.disclaimer: Check Waka Kotahi and regional councils for the latest fares and road user charges.
new-zealand.licenceBlurb: Conversion rules depend on where your licence was issued—some countries sit out of automatic conversion lists.

cities.name: Auckland
cities.licenceNote: Motorway peaks are sharp—try flexible start times.

cities.name: Wellington
cities.modeMix: Golden Mile walk + cable car + ferries
cities.licenceNote: Windy narrow roads reward defensive driving courses.

cities.name: Christchurch
cities.licenceNote: Bike lanes expanded post-quake—lights and visibility still essential.

canada.title: Transit passes, winter detours, and plates
canada.lead: Weekly numbers are mock totals mixing transit pass amortisation and modest fuel — winter adds Uber spikes.
canada.disclaimer: Provinces issue driver licences; cities set transit fares. Confirm before you budget.
canada.licenceBlurb: Snow tires or winter-rated tires are socially and legally expected in many provinces—budget install and storage.

officialLinks.label: Government of Canada — driving as a newcomer

cities.name: Toronto
cities.modeMix: TTC subway + streetcar + GO for sprawl
cities.licenceNote: GTA insurance premiums are notoriously high—get quotes early.

cities.name: Vancouver
cities.licenceNote: Bridge tolls mostly gone but fuel remains elevated.

cities.name: Montréal
cities.modeMix: STM Métro + bus + BIXI in summer
cities.licenceNote: Winter tyres and REM expansions change commute math—follow STM notices.

united-kingdom.title: Railcards, zones, and the left seat
united-kingdom.lead: Mock weekly totals assume a mix of TfL or regional rail plus occasional taxi when late — London is its own league.
united-kingdom.disclaimer: ULEZ, LEZ, and clean-air zones change often—check before you buy a car.
united-kingdom.licenceBlurb: Some licences swap for GB versions; others need theory and practical tests—DVLA guidance is definitive.

officialLinks.label: GOV.UK — drive in GB on a non-GB licence

cities.name: London
cities.licenceNote: Congestion and ULEZ sting if you drive daily—many migrants delay car ownership.

cities.name: Manchester
cities.licenceNote: Bee Network changes are rolling—watch stop relocations.

cities.name: Edinburgh
cities.licenceNote: Scotland sets its own licensing nuances—visit mygov.scot for detail.

global-pathways.title: How getting from A to B feels in each hub
global-pathways.lead: A qualitative snapshot for people comparing countries before they deep-dive a single city guide on each destination hub.
global-pathways.disclaimer: No fares quoted here—open the destination hub for illustrative weekly bands.
global-pathways.licenceBlurb: Every country on this site handles overseas licences differently—always read the official motor registry first.
